description: The middle of the day; when the sun is highest. 'Thaeso' names the day's peak—maximum light, fullest energy, the hinge between morning and afternoon.
sound_symbolism: The word stands at zenith. 'Tha' /θa/ begins with steady breath, presence established. The vowel 'e' opens to fullness. Then 'so' sustains at the peak, midday held.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua thaeso' (at midday). The center point of daylight hours, often a time for rest from labor in hot climates.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Midday rest preserved energy—working around the heat, pausing when the sun was strongest.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Midday offers peak solar energy—the time when sun-powered systems generate most.
